spec.description: Top-level health view. Service-health stats use Loki log volume over the dashboard time range as a liveness proxy (CloudWatch-based ECS metrics are deferred until cluster naming is standardized). Narrow the time picker to ~5m for strict liveness; widen to surface bursty local-dev activity. Each service stat drills into the per-service dashboard; each indexing stat drills into the Indexing dashboard.

panels.description: Which Grafana environment you are looking at. Rendered from the `env` constant template variable, which apply.sh substitutes to local / staging / production at push time. Designed to make staging vs production unmistakable at a glance.

Total realms (count of distinct realm_url in realm_meta) plus cumulative growth curve. The big number is the current total; the area sparkline shows the full history of when each realm was first indexed (min(indexed_at) per realm_url). Click to drill into the Realms dashboard for per-realm permissions, indexing status, and recent errors.

targets.rawSql: SELECT to_timestamp(min_at) AS time, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY min_at) AS "Realms" FROM (SELECT MIN(indexed_at) AS min_at FROM realm_meta GROUP BY realm_url) sub UNION ALL SELECT NOW(), (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT realm_url) FROM realm_meta) ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Realms

panels.description: Total registered users (count of users table) plus cumulative growth curve. Big number is the current total; sparkline shows the full history of user signups by users.created_at. Click to drill into the Users dashboard for credits, realm permissions, and access management.

targets.rawSql: SELECT to_timestamp(created_at) AS time, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY created_at) AS "Users" FROM users UNION ALL SELECT NOW(), (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users) ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Users

panels.description: Concurrent users right now: count of distinct Matrix users seen on the Synapse Sliding Sync endpoint within a rolling 5-minute window, evaluated across the dashboard time range. Big number is the latest 5-minute count; sparkline shows how the active-user count moved over the range. Sourced from Synapse access logs via Loki — matches `Processed request` lines hitting the MSC3575 Sliding Sync path (`simplified_msc3575/sync`) and extracts the `@user:server` token from the log prefix. Path is on the unstable MSC3575 namespace and may shift when sliding-sync stabilises upstream.

targets.expr: count(count by (user) (count_over_time({service="synapse"} |= "Processed request" |= "simplified_msc3575/sync" | regexp `\{(?P<user>@[^}]+:[^}]+)\}` [5m]))) or vector(0)

panels.title: Concurrent Users

panels.description: Matrix user IDs seen on the Synapse Sliding Sync endpoint within the last 5 minutes. Click a user to open the Users dashboard focused on them.

Realm-server HTTP throughput, errors, and target response time. Throughput / errors come from Loki (`httpLogging` exit lines `--> METHOD ACCEPT URL: STATUS` and any line matching `error|exception|fatal`). p50 / p95 / p99 come from the realm-server ALB's `AWS/ApplicationELB.TargetResponseTime` metric (CloudWatch ExtendedStatistic) on the right axis in seconds — locally these read 'No data' but throughput/errors keep working. Legend shows the mean over the visible time range.
panels.title: Web Requests

panels.description: Priority-pending worker jobs (status='unfulfilled' with no active reservation AND priority < 0). Sparkline shows resolved jobs/min over the dashboard time range, with the trailing point set to the current priority-pending count so `lastNotNull` matches the displayed value. Color thresholds: green ≤3, yellow >3, red >10. Counts ALL job types — for indexing-only depth see the Indexing dashboard.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(finished_at, '1m', 0),
       COUNT(*)::int AS "Pending priority jobs"
  FROM jobs
 WHERE status = 'resolved'
   AND finished_at IS NOT NULL
   AND $__timeFilter(finished_at)
 GROUP BY 1
UNION ALL
SELECT EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM NOW()),
       (SELECT COUNT(*)::int
          FROM jobs j
          LEFT JOIN job_reservations jr
            ON j.id = jr.job_id
           AND jr.completed_at IS NULL
           AND jr.locked_until > NOW()
         WHERE j.status = 'unfulfilled'
           AND j.priority < 0
           AND jr.id IS NULL)
 ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Job Queue

panels.description: Synapse send-event rate — `PUT /_matrix/client/.../send/<event-type>/<txnId>` requests parsed from synapse INFO-level access logs (`Processed request: ... "PUT .../send/... HTTP/1.1"`). Counts ALL Matrix event types (`m.room.message` for chat, `app.boxel.realm-event` for boxel realm sync, etc.) since boxel uses Matrix as its event bus, not just for chat. Legend shows the mean over the visible time range.
panels.title: Matrix Events

panels.description: ECS CPU and memory utilisation for `boxel-realm-server-${env}` on cluster `${env}` — averages over the panel refresh window. Task counts (Run / Need) appear in the stat row below this bargauge. Locally these show 'No data' — CloudWatch is staging/production only.
panels.title: Realm Server

panels.description: ECS task count for `boxel-realm-server-${env}` — `Run` is RunningTaskCount, `Need` is DesiredTaskCount (both from ECS/ContainerInsights, Maximum over the panel refresh window). Run < Need means the service is under-scaled or tasks are crashing.
panels.title: Tasks

panels.description: ECS CPU and memory utilisation for `boxel-prerender-server-${env}` on cluster `${env}` — averages over the panel refresh window. Task counts (Run / Need) appear in the stat row below this bargauge. Locally these show 'No data' — CloudWatch is staging/production only.
panels.title: Prerender

panels.description: ECS task count for `boxel-prerender-server-${env}` — `Run` is RunningTaskCount, `Need` is DesiredTaskCount (both from ECS/ContainerInsights, Maximum over the panel refresh window). Run < Need means the service is under-scaled or tasks are crashing.
panels.title: Tasks

panels.description: ECS CPU and memory utilisation for `boxel-prerender-manager-${env}` on cluster `${env}` — averages over the panel refresh window. Task counts (Run / Need) appear in the stat row below this bargauge. Locally these show 'No data' — CloudWatch is staging/production only.
panels.title: Prerender Mgr

panels.description: ECS task count for `boxel-prerender-manager-${env}` — `Run` is RunningTaskCount, `Need` is DesiredTaskCount (both from ECS/ContainerInsights, Maximum over the panel refresh window). Run < Need means the service is under-scaled or tasks are crashing.
panels.title: Tasks

panels.description: ECS CPU and memory utilisation for `boxel-worker-${env}` on cluster `${env}` — averages over the panel refresh window. Task counts (Run / Need) appear in the stat row below this bargauge. Locally these show 'No data' — CloudWatch is staging/production only.
panels.title: Worker

panels.description: ECS task count for `boxel-worker-${env}` — `Run` is RunningTaskCount, `Need` is DesiredTaskCount (both from ECS/ContainerInsights, Maximum over the panel refresh window). Run < Need means the service is under-scaled or tasks are crashing.
panels.title: Tasks

panels.description: Synapse process metrics scraped from /\_synapse/metrics via the synapse-prometheus datasource (local Prometheus locally; AMP in staging/production). CPU is rate over 5m as a percentage of one core; Mem is process_resident_memory_bytes.
panels.title: Synapse Process

panels.description: Boxel application database transaction rate (xact_commit + xact_rollback) averaged since the postmaster started. Not a rolling window — recent spikes will be smoothed. Drill into the DB-specific dashboard for detail.

targets.rawSql: SELECT 60.0 * (xact_commit + xact_rollback) / NULLIF(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - pg_postmaster_start_time())), 0) AS "txn/min" FROM pg_stat_database WHERE datname = 'boxel';

panels.title: Postgres DB

panels.description: All firing and pending Grafana alert rules. Drill into a rule for details and silences. Replaces the Worker Status alertlist (which was a single-panel dashboard).
panels.title: Active Alerts

panels.description: Indexing jobs (from-scratch-index, incremental-index, full-reindex) waiting on a worker. Click to open Indexing dashboard.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS pending FROM jobs j LEFT JOIN job_reservations jr ON j.id = jr.job_id AND jr.completed_at IS NULL AND jr.locked_until > NOW() WHERE j.status = 'unfulfilled' AND j.job_type IN ('from-scratch-index','incremental-index','full-reindex') AND jr.id IS NULL;

panels.title: Pending Indexing Jobs

panels.description: Indexing jobs currently being processed by a worker.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS in_flight FROM jobs j JOIN job_reservations jr ON j.id = jr.job_id AND jr.completed_at IS NULL AND jr.locked_until > NOW() WHERE j.finished_at IS NULL AND j.job_type IN ('from-scratch-index','incremental-index','full-reindex');

panels.title: In-flight

panels.description: Age of the oldest pending indexing job. Yellow at 1 minute, red at 5 minutes.

targets.rawSql: SELECT EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - MIN(j.created_at))) AS oldest_pending_seconds FROM jobs j LEFT JOIN job_reservations jr ON j.id = jr.job_id AND jr.completed_at IS NULL AND jr.locked_until > NOW() WHERE j.status = 'unfulfilled' AND j.job_type IN ('from-scratch-index','incremental-index','full-reindex') AND jr.id IS NULL;

panels.title: Oldest Pending

panels.description: Files in boxel_index whose latest index attempt failed (across all realms).

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS errors FROM boxel_index WHERE type = 'error' AND is_deleted IS NOT TRUE;

panels.title: Indexing Errors

panels.description: Indexing throughput across all realms in the dashboard time window. Drills into the Indexing dashboard.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(j.created_at, '30s'), COUNT(*) AS arrived FROM jobs j WHERE j.job_type IN ('from-scratch-index','incremental-index','full-reindex') AND $__timeFilter(j.created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(jr.created_at, '30s'), COUNT(*) AS started FROM job_reservations jr JOIN jobs j ON j.id = jr.job_id WHERE j.job_type IN ('from-scratch-index','incremental-index','full-reindex') AND $__timeFilter(jr.created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(j.finished_at, '30s'), COUNT(*) AS completed FROM jobs j WHERE j.job_type IN ('from-scratch-index','incremental-index','full-reindex') AND j.finished_at IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(j.finished_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Indexing throughput

options.content:
### Other dashboards

- **Entity dashboards** — pick a specific entity to inspect:
  - [Realms](/d/boxelrealms001) — counts, permissions, indexing status, recent errors
  - [Users](/d/boxelusers0001) — credits, realm permissions, grant access
- **Workflow dashboards**:
  - [Indexing](/d/aetquzjcf2znke) — pipeline metrics + operator actions
  - [Job Queue](/d/boxeljobqueue1) — generic worker queue (all job types)
- **Forensics**: [Logs](/d/fetquzizsej28b) — filter by service, user, realm
- **Service deep-dives** (stub — currently filtered logs only): [Realm Server](/d/boxel-svc-realm-server) · [Prerender Server](/d/boxel-svc-prerender-server) · [Prerender Manager](/d/boxel-svc-prerender-manager) · [Worker](/d/boxel-svc-worker) · [Synapse](/d/000000012)

spec.title: Overview
